message.content: The ocean appears blue primarily because water absorbs colors in the red part of the light spectrum and reflects and scatters the blue wavelengths. When sunlight enters the water, the longer red, orange, and yellow wavelengths are absorbed more quickly, while the shorter blue wavelengths are scattered and reflected back to our eyes. This scattering effect causes the ocean to look blue to observers on land, especially when the water is deep and clear. Additionally, the presence of tiny particles and algae can influence the exact shade of blue,
